research_gaps: Exact amount of DWF Labs investment (Jan 2024) not publicly disclosed
Staking minimum not specified in documentation
Phala Cloud revenue not separately disclosed from treasury spending
Legacy Builders Program status unclear post-migration to Ethereum L2
Exact last commit date on phala-blockchain repo (approximate: 2025-12-XX)
Logo URL assumed from phala.com — may differ

identity.name: Phala Network
identity.one_liner: Decentralised confidential computing cloud using hardware TEEs for private AI inference and agent execution
identity.chain: Ethereum L2 (Op-Succinct rollup via Conduit, migrated from Polkadot Nov 2025)

founding_team.name: Marvin Tong
founding_team.background: Ex-Tencent product manager, Didi senior product manager

founding_team.name: Hang Yin
founding_team.background: Ex-Google software engineer, Bitcoin Gold co-founder

team.entity_name: Hashforest Technology Pte. Ltd. (parent); Hashforest Technology LLC (subsidiary)

technical.architecture_summary: Three-layer system: (1) Ethereum L2 via Op-Succinct rollup with SP1 ZK proofs deployed through Conduit, (2) TEE compute layer running dstack Confidential VMs on Intel TDX and NVIDIA H100/H200/B200 GPUs, (3) Decentralised KMS using P2P with MPC key sharing. The L2 handles token transfers, staking, governance, and worker registration, while actual computation runs off-chain inside hardware TEEs with remote attestation.
technical.consensus_mechanism: Op-Succinct ZK rollup (validity proofs via SP1 zkVM)
technical.what_is_onchain: Token transfers, staking, governance votes (Snapshot), worker registration, attestation records
technical.what_is_offchain: TEE computation (confidential VMs), AI inference, agent execution, key management (MPC), GPU workloads

audits.scope: dstack + meta-dstack (remote attestation, key management, Yocto configuration)
Phat Contract Runtime (Pink Runtime, ink! contracts, 2711 LoC)

security_incidents.description: WireTap SGX Attack: Academic researchers extracted SGX keys in lab environment. No real-world exploitation, no funds lost. Phala shut down all SGX workers and migrated to Intel TDX. Only affects DDR4 systems.
Worker Node Cloning: Theoretical enclave cloning vulnerability identified by Ruhr University Bochum. Low severity, no data loss, Phala Cloud unaffected. Fixed with sync metadata.

tokenomics.token_standard: ERC-20 (Ethereum) + native on legacy Phala parachain

emissions.burn_mechanism: Unmined PHA burned before mainnet; no ongoing burn

staking.type: PHA → vPHA liquid staking on Ethereum
staking.slashing: Gatekeepers can be slashed; GPU workers removed for non-compliance

tokenomics.classification: Disinflationary utility token with compute-backed demand
tokenomics.notes: CoinCarp reports 961M circulating vs Etherscan 831M — discrepancy likely due to CoinCarp counting legacy parachain tokens. All investor vesting fully complete. DWF Labs is both investor and market maker.

methods.description: Stake PHA for vPHA via liquid staking on Ethereum
Run GPU worker node with Intel TDX CPU and NVIDIA H100/H200/B200 GPU
methods.barriers: Intel TDX CPU required, NVIDIA CC GPU required, email registration (not self-service)

methods.description: Deploy applications on Phala Cloud using dstack and Docker
Use Phala Cloud for confidential AI inference (H200 $3.50/hr on-demand)

developer_resources.grants_programme: Legacy Builders Program (up to $50K, status unclear post-migration); Monthly Builders Challenge ($2,500 prizes)

usage_metrics.daily_active_users_note: On-chain addresses on legacy parachain (Dec 2024) — mostly worker nodes, not human users
usage_metrics.revenue_note: Partially verified — treasury spending was $2.1M in 2024 but Cloud revenue not separately disclosed

community.governance_type: Snapshot + Safe multisig on Ethereum L1 (migrated from Polkadot on-chain governance)
community.sentiment: Moderately bullish; developer-focused; strong Chinese-language community

infrastructure_decentralisation.evidence: L2 Stage 0 with centralised sequencer, permissioned proposer, instant-upgradable contracts. GPU worker registration requires emailing the team (not self-service). 87.4% single-developer concentration on dstack (kvinwang). 29,478 active devices provides hardware distribution but chain-level infrastructure is heavily centralised.

governance_decentralisation.evidence: Snapshot + Safe multisig controlled by team. Governance downgraded from Polkadot on-chain system with council/referenda to off-chain Snapshot voting. No evidence of community override ability. Forum exists (617+ topics) but decision-making is team-led.

token_distribution_fairness.evidence: 70% allocated to mining/compute rewards is genuinely community-oriented. 5% team allocation is low. However, 15% private sale had zero lock-up (100% at TGE). All investor vesting now fully complete. 83.1% circulating. DWF Labs dual role as investor and market maker is a concern.

censorship_resistance.evidence: TEE provides genuine computation-level censorship resistance — workloads inside enclaves cannot be inspected or modified. However, centralised sequencer is a chokepoint for L2 transactions. GPU onboarding is gated (email-based). California ToS apply via Hashforest Technology LLC subsidiary.

data_sovereignty.evidence: Strongest dimension. TEE ensures zero-knowledge of user data during computation. Remote attestation is verifiable. dstack enables self-hosted deployment for maximum sovereignty. SOC 2 Type I and HIPAA compliance certifications. Gaps: sequencer has metadata visibility, Cloud registration requires account creation.

open_source_and_transparency.evidence: Apache-2.0 licensed. dstack donated to Linux Foundation for neutral governance. Two professional audits (zkSecurity, Code4rena) plus token audit. Annual transparency reports published (2024 Year in Review, 2025 Report). Held back by instant-upgradable L2 contracts without timelock and single-developer concentration on core repo.

freedom_score.freedom_summary: Phala's TEE architecture delivers genuinely strong data sovereignty and privacy guarantees at the computation layer. However, the migration to an Ethereum L2 has introduced significant centralisation across infrastructure, governance, and censorship resistance. The gap between Phala's privacy promise and its current chain-level decentralisation is the core tension.

what_works_today: TEE-based confidential computing is real and differentiated — genuinely private AI inference
SOC 2 Type I and HIPAA compliance position the project for enterprise adoption
ElizaOS V2 integration and NEAR partnership provide real usage channels
398 paid users and $2M+ claimed ARR show early commercial traction
dstack donation to Linux Foundation is a credibility signal for open-source commitment
Proactive WireTap response (shutting down all SGX workers, migrating to TDX) showed competent security handling
what_is_hype: '758,000 daily agent executions' was on the now-retired parachain, not the current L2
'35,000+ workers' are legacy SGX miners, most decommissioned; current active is 29,478 but L2 has near-zero activity
'$2M ARR' is partially verified — treasury spending was $2.1M but Cloud revenue not separately disclosed
'Decentralised' cloud is Stage 0 with centralised sequencer and team-controlled upgrades
42,900 'DAU' are on-chain addresses (mostly worker nodes), not human users
key_risks: L2 Stage 0 — instant-upgradable contracts with no exit window; users trust the operator entirely
87.4% single-developer concentration on dstack (kvinwang) — extreme bus factor
Hardware vendor dependency on Intel TDX and NVIDIA Confidential Computing
Very low L2 TVL ($633K) suggests limited real economic activity on current chain
Multiple simultaneous migrations (SGX→TDX, Polkadot→Ethereum, Phat Contracts→dstack) create execution risk
DWF Labs as both investor and market maker
competitive_advantages: Only TEE cloud with SOC 2 Type I and HIPAA compliance certifications
First TEE platform for ElizaOS V2 agent swarms
dstack in Linux Foundation provides neutral governance for core infrastructure
Multi-hardware TEE support (Intel TDX + NVIDIA Confidential Computing)
Real enterprise compliance certifications differentiate from narrative-only competitors
competitive_disadvantages: L2 significantly less decentralised than competitors (Stage 0)
Small paid user base (398) vs. larger compute networks
Low TVL ($633K) signals limited DeFi/economic activity
Heavy dependency on two hardware vendors (Intel, NVIDIA)
Governance downgraded from on-chain (Polkadot) to Snapshot — a regression
assessment.outlook: Phala has genuine technology in TEE-based confidential computing and is making a credible push into enterprise AI infrastructure. The SOC 2 + HIPAA certifications and ElizaOS integration differentiate it from pure narrative plays. However, the L2 is very early (Stage 0) and the project faces execution risk from multiple simultaneous platform migrations. Success depends on whether the enterprise compliance positioning converts to meaningful paid adoption beyond the current 398 users.
assessment.overall: A technically credible confidential computing platform with real enterprise ambitions, held back by significant L2 centralisation and early-stage commercial traction. Better classified as DeAI-adjacent (general-purpose confidential compute pivoting to AI) than Core DeAI.
